name: Redd Foxx
bio: Redd Foxx was an American stand-up comedian and actor known as the King of the Party Records for his raunchy nightclub act and dozens of best-selling blue comedy albums. He reached his widest fame as the cantankerous Fred G. Sanford on the 1970s sitcom Sanford and Son, work that earned a Golden Globe and helped redefine the Black sitcom.

timeline.ev: Performs on the Major Bowes Amateur Hour radio show as a teenager
Records early comedy albums for Dootone, becoming a party-record favorite
Stars as Fred G. Sanford on Sanford and Son
Headlines his own ABC variety series, Redd Foxx
Returns to TV in The Redd Foxx Show

accolades: Golden Globe Award for Sanford and Son, with three additional nominations
Three Primetime Emmy nominations for Sanford and Son
Known as the King of the Party Records with more than 50 albums
Posthumous star on the St. Louis Walk of Fame (1992)
